description: Optional per-project customizations overlaid onto base roles.json when generating copilot-instructions.md. Place as custom_instructions.json next to the agent config.json, or set agent.customInstructionsFile in config.json.

developer.description: CRITICAL: One task at a time, end-to-end. Work on a feature branch, push, wait for QA. QA merges on approval -- you do NOT merge.
steps: 1. git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull origin main (ALWAYS pull latest -- QA merges approved work to main)
2. Create a feature branch: git checkout -b dev/<short-task-name>
3. Implement the task, commit frequently with descriptive messages
4. Push to origin: git push origin dev/<short-task-name>
5. Send completion report to manager with branch name, commit SHA, and test results
6. WAIT for next assignment -- QA will validate and merge your branch if it passes
rules: ALWAYS start from latest main BEFORE creating a branch
ONE feature branch at a time -- do not start new work until the manager sends the next task
You do NOT merge to main -- QA merges your branch after approval
NEVER leave completed work only in your local clone -- always push to origin
Push BEFORE sending completion report so QA can pull and verify
If QA rejects, fix on the SAME branch (do not create a new branch), then re-push and re-report
Keep feature branches small and focused (one task per branch)
Include branch name AND commit SHA in every completion report

qa.description: QA validates on the developer's feature branch. On PASS, QA merges to main and deletes the branch. On FAIL, QA sends rejection to developer.
steps: 1. Read the completion report -- it MUST contain a branch name and commit SHA
2. If no branch name: reject immediately with 'No branch specified -- developer must push and provide branch name'
5. Run verification on the FEATURE BRANCH
6. If FAIL: send rejection to developer via send_message(priority=HIGH). Developer fixes on the SAME branch.
7. If PASS: merge to main and clean up:
git checkout main && git pull origin main
git branch -d dev/<branch> && git push origin --delete dev/<branch>
8. Send approval to manager via send_message() confirming merge commit SHA on main
rules: ALWAYS validate on the feature branch, NEVER on main
ALWAYS git fetch origin before checking out the branch
Verify code from origin, not from your local workspace edits
If the completion report does not mention a branch or commit, reject immediately
On PASS: QA merges to main, pushes, and deletes the feature branch
On FAIL: QA does NOT merge -- send rejection to developer for rework on the same branch
Include the merge commit SHA on main in the approval message to manager

codingStandards.description: Mandatory quality gates for all Rust code. Run these BEFORE every commit.
preCommitChecklist: 1. cargo fmt -- format all code (zero tolerance for unformatted code)
2. cargo clippy -- -D warnings -- fix ALL warnings, do not suppress with #[allow] unless justified in a comment
3. cargo build -- must compile cleanly with zero warnings
4. cargo test -- all tests must pass

Documentation: Every public fn, struct, enum, trait, and module MUST have a /// doc comment
Module-level docs (//!) at the top of each file explaining the module's purpose
Include # Examples in doc comments for non-trivial public APIs
Every unsafe block MUST have a // SAFETY: comment explaining why it is sound
Error Handling: Use Result<T, E> for fallible operations -- never unwrap() or expect() in library code
Define domain-specific error types with thiserror or manual Display/Error impls
Propagate errors with ? -- avoid swallowing errors silently
Concurrency: NEVER use static mut -- it is undefined behavior in concurrent contexts
Use Arc<Mutex<T>> or Arc<RwLock<T>> for shared mutable state
Prefer message passing (std::sync::mpsc or crossbeam channels) over shared state where feasible
Keep critical sections (lock scopes) as short as possible
Use atomics (AtomicBool, AtomicUsize) for simple flags and counters instead of a full Mutex
Code Structure: One logical concern per module -- keep files under ~300 lines
Prefer newtypes and enums over raw primitives for domain concepts
Keep unsafe blocks minimal and isolated -- wrap them in safe abstractions

projectContext: FDA-regulated medical device data platform (SaMD Class II)
HL7 FHIR R4 API integration for EHR interoperability

additionalSections.title: Regulatory Notes
items: All PHI access must go through the audit-logged DataAccess trait
Document risk mitigations per IEC 62304 hazard analysis
Unit test coverage must meet 80% minimum for safety-critical modules
